parsed_verdict.reasoning: The rule set contains 10 top-level rules in both Stage 1 and Stage 2 (same numbering), well within the N9 parsimony threshold. No two rules describe the same phenomenon — each addresses a distinct aspect (natural place, weight, medium resistance, surface/shape, upward-belonging, floating, forced motion, pull-vs-weight, carried-push, heavens). All rules trace to specific observations (cited inline) and use permitted Aristotelian elaborations (natural place, medium resistance, fading impressed-motion/antiperistasis). Hierarchy is satisfied by explicit cross-references such as 'Combining with Rule 2', 'see Rule 10', 'handled by Rule 9', and 'Rule 8'.
